item.title: Accenture forecasts quarterly revenue below estimates as clients delay enterprise spending
item.description: Company expects a revenue hit of 1% this year as firms adjust their plans amid war in the Middle East

item.title: U.S. regulators set to approve new rules softening capital requirements for banks
item.description: Changes would follow a yearslong campaign from Wall Street firms to ease rules introduced after the 2008 financial crisis

item.title: The dynamic globalisation machine will overcome the Iran shock
item.description: Short-term crisis management in shipping should not be mistaken for fatal weakness in trade Alan Beattie

item.title: Deloitte tax boss put forward as only candidate in UK leadership vote
item.description: Darren Graves will become Big Four firm’s new chief if approved by colleagues

item.title: Paris appeals court rejects France’s bid to suspend Shein’s marketplace
item.description: Childlike sex dolls and banned weapons were found for sale on the platform in November, prompting the government action

item.title: How (not) to help with high energy prices
item.description: Heed the lessons from 2022

item.title: European Central Bank expected to hold rate, signal readiness to raise as inflation fears mount
item.description: Financial markets expect inflation to climb above 3% over the next year, with traders betting on two rate hikes by December

item.title: FirstFT: Energy prices surge after Iran strikes Qatari LNG site
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: The Federal Reserve holds rates as energy prices rise and Delcy Rodríguez overhauls her cabinet

item.title: Business Brief: Trumped
item.description: Also in today’s edition: Canada’s population is shrinking

item.title: Separatists think independence makes Quebec and Alberta richer. Really?
item.description: The economic case for breaking up Canada is weaker than its advocates admit, as going it alone would leave Quebec and Alberta poorer, not freer

item.title: Iran hits Gulf refineries after Israel strikes its offshore gas field
item.description: Gulf states condemn attacks on Saudi, Qatari and Kuwaiti energy facilities

item.title: AI chatbots often encourage delusions and self-harm, study finds
item.description: The post AI chatbots often encourage delusions and self-harm, study finds appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Iran inflicts ‘extensive damage’ on site of world’s largest LNG facility in Qatar
item.description: Tehran vowed to retaliate against Gulf energy plants after Israel struck its South Pars gasfield

item.title: Chair of India’s largest private bank quits over ‘ethical differences’
item.description: Central bank calms market saying ‘there are no material concerns’ about the health of HDFC Bank

item.title: Submit your questions to Katie Martin: are markets heading for a shock?
item.description: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with the FT’s markets columnist today at 1pm GMT

item.title: A test for chip testing and SoftBank’s stumble
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: The Fed can only wait and wonder
item.description: Plus, the stock market’s wartime reversal

item.title: Middle East war gatecrashes EU leaders’ economy summit
item.description: Also in this newsletter: DHL Express Europe looks on the bright side

item.title: Kirkland & Ellis, the gilded age
item.description: The firm becomes first to break $10bn in annual revenues while equity partners take home $11.1mn

item.title: Spain is a role model in weathering Iranian oil shocks
item.description: The country’s speedy rollout of renewables has put a lid on electricity bills

item.title: BrewDog’s new owner seeks female drinkers to revive brewer
item.description: Irwin Simon said he ‘would love’ to see more women drinkers and dismissed health concerns over moderate alcohol consumption

item.title: Why bank cards may be heading the way of the cheque book
item.description: The £100 cap on contactless card spending is lifted today — but digital wallets matter more for future growth

item.title: Scale is not everything in asset management
item.description: Seeking size for its own sake can distract fund managers from focusing on clients and shareholders

item.title: When it comes to your pension, don’t believe the hype about private equity
item.description: Individual investors should be wary of the claims made — especially when they come from politicians

item.title: Trump sails into battle with Lloyd’s of London
item.description: The market’s historic strength in marine war insurance is being challenged by the US

item.title: Economists have caught the AI bug
item.description: It’s expanding the scope of research but challenging the profession’s process of weeding out mistakes

item.title: US exploring tying naval escorts in Strait of Hormuz to government insurance
item.description: Vessels seeking protection may be required to take out cover from programme run by Development Finance Corporation

item.title: Apple supplier Murata starts US-China rare earths decoupling
item.description: Japanese manufacturer joins companies rushing to insulate themselves from geopolitical risk

item.title: Tired of sports betting? Take a punt on babies
item.description: A new generation of gambling games can change the way we think about the structures governing our world

item.title: Indonesia faces fuel fears as half its population travels for Eid
item.description: More than 140mn people expected to travel, putting pressure on energy demand as war in Middle East pushes oil above $100 a barrel

item.title: Elliott Management and the art of telling bosses they’re wrong
item.description: The hedge fund has industrialised shareholder activism

item.title: UK to protect steel industry with new import tariffs
item.description: Move brings Britain in line with US, EU and Canada which have sought to bolster domestic producers

item.title: Gas price surges 25% and oil hits $115 as strikes rattle markets
item.description: Donald Trump threatens massive attack on Iranian gasfield if Tehran continues to target Qatari facilities

item.title: Canaccord reworks U.S. leadership in wake of regulatory settlement
item.description: Dan Daviau will temporarily oversee U.S. division while dealer finds successor to retiring Jeff Barlow

item.title: Metatek IPO prices below marketing range, reflecting soft investor demand
item.description: Tech company priced its TSX IPO at $5 per share, raising a total of $40-million, but had hoped to price the deal between $5.75 and $6.25 per share

item.title: Fines for false made-in-Canada claims could chill investment, food manufacturers say
item.description: CFIA is responding to concerns that retail giants are falsely capitalizing on the buy-Canadian movement

item.title: US relaxes sanctions on Venezuela’s PDVSA amid global energy squeeze
item.description: Licence allows nation’s state oil group to sell directly to American companies and on international market

item.title: Middle East war day 19 as it happened: Oil prices briefly top $110 after attacks on Gulf energy facilities
item.description: Qatar reports ‘extensive damage’ on site of world’s largest LNG plant Ras Laffan after Iran strike

item.title: Chinese EV maker BYD in talks to open Canadian dealerships, consultant says
item.description: Automaker said to be looking at establishing 20 dealerships in GTA, followed by locations in three other cities

item.title: What if Donald Trump decided to ban oil exports?
item.description: Trying to keep prices low that way could backfire spectacularly

item.title: Saskatchewan budget avoids tax hikes but projects $819-million deficit
item.description: Province plans to spend nearly $1.2-billion extra, mostly on health care, education, social services and policing

item.title: Quebec projects $8.6-billion deficit in election-year budget
item.description: Province’s budget focuses on core services like health and education with no flashy big-ticket spending

item.title: Russia considers sending armed naval patrols to protect ‘shadow fleet’
item.description: Putin ally Patrushev says Moscow could deploy ‘mobile firing groups’ after series of suspected Ukrainian attacks on ships

item.title: Oil prices rise on Iranian attack against Persian Gulf energy producers
item.description: International benchmark crude oil jumped to nearly US$110 a barrel during the session, before paring gains

item.title: Teck royalty on Fourmile gold project could be worth billions, potentially affecting Barrick’s IPO plans
item.description: Barrick has not yet publicly disclosed the royalty in a project it has described as ‘one of the century’s greatest gold discoveries’

item.title: How Zara fought off H&M and Shein
item.description: A touch of luxury has helped

item.title: America’s war on Iran is a gift to Vladimir Putin
item.description: Ukraine’s other allies need to limit the windfall to Moscow

item.title: Lex Greensill fails to persuade court to throw out UK director ban case
item.description: Australian financier must go to trial over fate after Insolvency Service launched probe

item.title: BofA pitches bets against European private credit
item.description: US bank told clients that European stocks exposed to private credit had 30 per cent ‘downside risk’

item.title: Silver linings to the heavy cloud of higher energy prices
item.description: Natural gas consumption has fallen and resilience has improved across Europe and in the UK

item.title: How hard will war hit the Gulf’s economies?
item.description: The highly globalised UAE has felt the impact of conflict more than insular Saudi Arabia

item.title: Store traffic expected to rise across U.S. Circle K stores despite surging gasoline prices, Couche-Tard CEO says
item.description: Retail giant expects sales momentum to continue despite war in the Middle East

item.title: Uncertainty reigns for central bank as Iran war rages
item.description: The central bank will have to weigh the inflation risks of high oil prices against the economic damage of trade uncertainty

item.title: Canada reports first annual population decline on record
item.description: Population fell by more than 100,000 people last year amid an exodus of temporary residents

item.title: BoC holds benchmark rate at 2.25% amid oil price shock
item.description: Macklem says the central bank is ready to adjust policy if needed as inflation risks emerge

item.title: Stock markets Wednesday: Major indexes close near lows as oil prices rise and traders pare U.S. rate cut bets after Fed decision
item.description: Wall Street, TSX lower as BoC and Fed keep rates steady

item.title: Federal Reserve holds rates steady, projects single rate cut for 2026
item.description: Fed officials expect Iran war will worsen inflation while having little impact on economic growth

item.title: How Canada can help allies with the Iran oil shock: The answer may surprise you
item.description: Investing in geothermal energy could have a domino effect

item.title: Rumours of 1970s stagflation from Iran oil shock are greatly exaggerated
item.description: There are some key differences between conditions today and in the 1970s

item.description: The post Canadian scientist Gilles Brassard wins Turing Award for quantum breakthroughs appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Ottawa spending $1.4-billion to ramp up domestic ammunition production capacity
item.description: Funds will be divided between IMT Precision and General Dynamics Ordnance and Tactical Systems

item.title: Trump has broken it. Now he owns it
item.description: Continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz threatens the movement of gas and fertilisers as well as oil Martin Wolf

item.title: UAE set to show leniency on tax rules for expats leaving to avoid Iran conflict
item.description: Move would be particularly important for Dubai, which has attracted wealthy individuals with its zero income tax rate Tax

item.title: Nvidia is expanding its empire
item.description: The AI boom’s biggest winner moves beyond chips

item.title: Will South Korea’s epic bull market survive the energy shock?
item.description: Probably, but investors should brace for more volatility

item.title: China cannot escape the energy shock
item.description: Despite renewables and reserves, it will suffer

item.title: Shopify’s AI-powered loans business is growing fast
item.description: The post Shopify’s AI-powered loans business is growing fast appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Spain warns EU against suspending carbon market to try to lower energy prices
item.description: Energy minister says suspending ETS in response to oil price surge would be a ‘big error’

item.title: Washington seeks to reassure sovereign wealth funds over tax changes
item.description: Top foreign investors have warned they could cut their US exposure if Treasury presses ahead Tax

item.title: Trapped in their starter homes: Canada has a second-time homebuyer problem
item.description: Younger people hoping to upsize for a growing family face a bigger challenge than the last generation

item.title: The Iran war is roiling commodities far beyond oil
item.description: Shortages of fuels and chemicals threaten industries from farming to pharmaceuticals

item.title: War may bring lasting change to the airline business
item.description: Western carriers will relish the chance to win some of their customers back

item.title: Does Ottawa think simply creating a new financial crime agency solves everything?
item.description: Expecting a new agency with around 150 employees to assume the mandate is impractical and naive

item.title: Labour must not lose my generation of voters
item.description: The defeat by the Greens in Greater Manchester demonstrates the danger of failing to address economic burdens Tax

item.title: U.S. venture investors are backing fewer Canadian deals, bucking a years-long trend
item.description: The post U.S. venture investors are backing fewer Canadian deals, bucking a years-long trend appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Donald Trump starts putting his new tariff wall together
item.description: The administration is preparing to impose Section 301 duties supposedly against unfair trade Alan Beattie
